title: Marine and waterways transport operatives
description: Attend to live farm, ranch, open range or aquacultural animals that may include cattle, sheep, swine, goats, horses and other equines, poultry, rabbits, finfish, shellfish, and bees. Attend to animals produced for animal products, such as meat, fur, skins, feathers, eggs, milk, and honey. Duties may include feeding, watering, herding, grazing, milking, castrating, branding, de-beaking, weighing, catching, and loading animals. May maintain records on animals; examine animals to detect diseases and injuries; assist in birth deliveries; and administer medications, vaccinations, or insecticides as appropriate. May clean and maintain animal housing areas. Includes workers who shear wool from sheep and collect eggs in hatcheries. Hunt, trap, catch, or gather wild animals or aquatic animals and plants. May use nets, traps, or other equipment. May haul catch onto ship or other vessel. Assemble, install, repair, or maintain electric or hydraulic freight or passenger elevators, escalators, or dumbwaiters. (Based on 30 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Overhaul lifeboats or lifeboat gear and lower or raise lifeboats with winches or falls.
Issue directions for loading, unloading, and seating in boats; secure boats to docks with mooring lines, and cast off lines to enable departure.
Attach hoses and operate pumps to transfer substances to and from liquid cargo tanks; operate or maintain off-loading liquid pumps or valves.

Maintain a ship's engines under the direction of the ship's engineering officers; perform general marine vessel maintenance or repair work, such as repairing leaks, finishing interiors, refueling, or maintaining decks.
Relay specified signals to other ships, using visual signaling devices, such as blinker lights or semaphores.
Tie barges together into tow units for tugboats to handle, inspecting barges periodically during voyages and disconnecting them when destinations are reached.
Clean and polish wood trim, brass, or other metal parts; sweep, mop, and wash down decks to remove oil, dirt, and debris, using brooms, mops, brushes, and hoses.
Maintain desired courses, using compasses or electronic navigational aids; operate engine throttles and steering mechanisms to guide boats on desired courses.
Follow safety procedures to ensure the protection of passengers, cargo, and vessels; direct safety operations in emergency situations; lower and man lifeboats when emergencies occur.

Monitor engine, machinery, or equipment indicators when vessels are underway, and report abnormalities to appropriate shipboard staff; observe and interpret readings on gauges, meters, and charts registering various aspects of boiler operation to ensure that boilers are operating properly.

Maintain equipment such as range markers, fire extinguishers, boat fenders, lines, pumps, and fittings; monitor the availability, use, or condition of lifesaving equipment or pollution preventatives to ensure that international regulations are followed.

Arrange repairs, fuel, and supplies for vessels; oversee operation of vessels used for carrying passengers, motor vehicles, or goods across rivers, harbors, lakes, and coastal waters; operate, maintain, or repair ship equipment, such as winches, cranes, derricks, or weapons systems.

Organize and direct the activities of crew members; supervise the work of assistant stationary engineers, turbine operators, boiler tenders, or air conditioning and refrigeration operators and mechanics.

Clean boats and repair hulls and superstructures, using hand tools, paint, and brushes; chip and clean rust spots on decks, superstructures, or sides of ships, using wire brushes and hand or air chipping machines.

Examine machinery to verify specified pressures or lubricant flows; perform or arrange for repairs, such as complete overhauls, replacement of defective valves, gaskets, or bearings, or fabrication of new parts.

Read pressure and temperature gauges or displays and record data in engineering logs; maintain daily logs of operation, maintenance, and safety activities, including test results, instrument readings, and details of equipment malfunctions and maintenance work.
Give directions to crew members engaged in cleaning wheelhouses or quarterdecks; monitor boiler water, chemical, and fuel levels, and make adjustments to maintain required levels.
Perform or participate in emergency drills, as required; analyze problems and take appropriate action to ensure continuous and reliable operation of equipment and systems.

Test boiler water quality or arrange for testing and take necessary corrective action, such as adding chemicals to prevent corrosion and harmful deposits; maintain electrical power, heating, ventilation, refrigeration, water, or sewerage systems.
Handle lines to moor vessels to wharfs, tie up vessels to other vessels, or rig towing lines; drive trucks, tractors, and other equipment to distribute feed to animals.
Inspect, maintain, and repair equipment, machinery, buildings, pens, yards, and fences; clean stalls, pens, and equipment, using disinfectant solutions, brushes, shovels, water hoses, or pumps.
Feed and water livestock and monitor food and water supplies; herd livestock to pastures for grazing or to scales, trucks, or other enclosures.

Provide medical treatment, such as administering medications and vaccinations, or arrange for veterinarians to provide more extensive treatment; examine animals to detect illness, injury, or disease, and to check physical characteristics, such as rate of weight gain.
Record data in ships' logs, such as weather conditions or distances traveled; load or unload materials, vehicles, or passengers from vessels.

Inspect installation sites and study work orders, building plans, and installation manuals to determine materials requirements and installation procedures; perform duties related to livestock reproduction, such as breeding animals within appropriate timeframes, performing artificial inseminations, and helping with animal births.
Oversee the purchase of supplies, gear, and equipment; issue directions for loading, unloading, and seating in boats.
Participate in animal damage control, wildlife management, disease control, and research activities; protect herds from predators, using trained dogs.
Adjust controls and/or valves on equipment to provide power, and to regulate and set operations of system or industrial processes.
Transport fish to processing plants or to buyers; obtain required approvals for using poisons or traps, and notify persons in areas where traps and poison are set.
Demonstrate systems for customers and explain details, such as the causes and consequences of false alarms; advise customers on proper installation of valves or regulators and related equipment.
Load or unload materials, vehicles, or passengers from vessels; maintain engines, fishing gear, and other on-board equipment and perform minor repairs.

Install, inspect, maintain, and repair various equipment including electric meters, elevators, escalators, and automated door openers. This includes disassembling defective units and making repairs or replacements as necessary. Record maintenance information, including repairs performed and parts used, and provide customers with cost estimates for services.

Test, adjust and calibrate equipment to ensure proper functioning, including checking for leaks, faulty connections, and operational efficiency. Maintain logs and paperwork detailing repairs, services, and parts used.

Lubricate, clean, and maintain equipment to ensure smooth operation, including moving parts in electric meters, elevators, and appliances. This may involve using specialized tools and cleaning compounds.
Troubleshoot and investigate equipment malfunctions by examining parts, following wiring diagrams, and utilizing testing tools to diagnose issues.
Replace worn or broken parts in various equipment, such as motors, electrical connections, and door mechanisms, ensuring compliance with safety regulations and operational standards.
Assemble, install, and fasten mechanical parts such as wiring, doors, and tracks in accordance with blueprints and schematics to ensure proper equipment function.
Provide customer service by interacting with clients to diagnose equipment issues, explaining repair processes, and collecting payments upon job completion.
Drive motor vehicles to job sites as required, ensuring proper transportation of tools and equipment necessary for repair work.
Participate in training to enhance skills and knowledge related to equipment maintenance and repair, keeping up to date with new developments in the industry.

Maintain, repair, and overhaul farm machinery and vehicles, such as tractors, harvesters, and irrigation systems. Conduct routine maintenance, including cleaning, lubricating, and performing repairs on equipment and vehicles.

Dismantle machinery and equipment for inspection and repair, using hand tools, and reassemble after the completion of repair or maintenance work. Record details of repairs made and parts used, and inventory necessary machine parts and supplies.

Diagnose equipment malfunctions through visual inspection and testing, using various diagnostic tools and test equipment. Consult manuals, schematics, and engineering personnel to troubleshoot and solve equipment problems.

Test and inspect mechanical, hydraulic, and electrical systems, and components for defects or malfunctions. Evaluate performance characteristics, and ensure compliance with manufacturers' specifications after repairs or assembly.
Work collaboratively with other technicians or workers to repair, install, or maintain machinery and equipment, coordinating efforts to ensure effective operations and project completion.

Provide customer service by consulting with customers to describe equipment malfunctions and prepare cost estimates for repairs. Supply guidance on routine equipment maintenance and ensure satisfaction with service performed.
Operate, demonstrate, and deliver equipment to customers, ensuring proper function and addressing any operational issues.
Manage tool and spare parts inventory, requisitioning as necessary to maintain operational readiness of equipment and machinery.
Develop and modify specifications for industrial machines and equipment, including building foundations, assembling, testing, and ensuring accurate layout and installation of equipment.
Perform scheduled preventive maintenance tasks as outlined, including testing machinery operations and calibrating equipment to prescribed specifications.

Clean machines and machine parts, using cleaning solvents, cloths, air guns, hoses, vacuums, or other equipment. Clean, lubricate, or adjust parts, equipment, or machinery. Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure that they are safe, free of defects, and consistent with specifications. Inspect, test, and measure completed work, using devices such as hand tools or gauges to verify conformance to standards or repair requirements. Monitor employees' work levels and review work performance. Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards in areas such as cleanliness or maintenance.

Replace or repair metal, wood, leather, glass, or other lining in machines or in equipment compartments or containers. Repair or replace defective or worn parts such as bearings, pistons, and gears, using hand tools, torque wrenches, power tools, and welding equipment. Repair, fabricate, and install steel or wood fittings, using blueprints, shop sketches, and instruction manuals. Repair or replace broken or malfunctioning components of machinery or equipment. Dismantle machines and remove parts for repair, using hand tools, chain falls, jacks, cranes, or hoists. Disassemble machinery or equipment to remove parts and make repairs. Reassemble equipment after completion of inspections, testing, or repairs. Analyze test results, machine error messages, or information obtained from operators to diagnose equipment problems. Observe and test the operation of machinery or equipment to diagnose malfunctions, using voltmeters or other testing devices. Perform scheduled maintenance, and clean units and components.

Measure, mix, prepare, and test chemical solutions used to clean or repair machinery and equipment. Record repairs and maintenance performed. Maintain equipment service records. Record parts or materials used and order or requisition new parts or materials, as necessary. Record production, repair, and machine maintenance information. Requisition materials and supplies, such as tools, equipment, or replacement parts.

Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or to assist workers. Remove worn or damaged plastic block refractory linings of furnaces, using hand tools. Enforce safety rules and regulations. Confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or to resolve problems. Recommend or implement personnel actions, such as employee selection, evaluation, rewards, or disciplinary actions. Counsel employees about work-related issues and assist employees to correct job-skill deficiencies. Follow safety procedures during scheduled maintenance and inspections.

Participate in budget preparation and administration, coordinating purchasing and documentation and monitoring departmental expenditures. Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures. Meet with vendors or suppliers to discuss products used in repair work. Monitor tool and part inventories and the condition and maintenance of shops to ensure adequate working conditions. Plan work assignments and equipment allocations to meet transportation, operations or production goals.

Inspect the interior and exterior of rail cars coming into rail yards to identify defects and to determine the extent of wear and damage. Examine parts for defects, such as breakage or excessive wear. Determine schedules, sequences, and assignments for work activities, based on work priority, quantity of equipment, and skill of personnel. Analyze and record personnel or operational data and write related activity reports. Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations. Direct workers in transportation or related services, such as pumping, moving, storing, or loading or unloading of materials.

Assist workers in tasks, such as loading vehicles. Train workers in proper operational procedures and functions and explain company policies. Explain and demonstrate work tasks to new workers or assign training tasks to experienced workers. Conduct or arrange for worker training in safety, repair, or maintenance techniques, operational procedures, or equipment use. Recommend or implement measures to improve worker motivation, equipment performance, work methods, or customer services.

Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials. Inspect components such as bearings, seals, gaskets, wheels, and coupler assemblies to determine if repairs are needed. Reline or repair ladles and pouring spouts with refractory clay, using trowels. Conferring with personnel, such as management, engineering, quality control, customer, or union workers' representatives, to coordinate work activities, resolve employee grievances, or identify and review resource needs. Estimate costs of repairs based on parts and labor requirements. Repair and maintain electrical and electronic controls for propulsion and braking systems. Extrapolate actual costs of factors such as materials, labor, or outside contractors. Meet with employees to discuss work performance and recommend necessary adjustments or improvements.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments or to assist workers.
